creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
videos.summary: Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard present an approach to convert notes, documents, videos, and repositories into memory representations for AI agents. The technique enables agents to leverage stored information for reasoning and decision-making.

videos.title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
videos.summary: OpenGov's Gabe De Mesa discusses production deployment of AI agents serving state and local governments. The talk covers agent architecture, A2A protocol, Effect-TS tooling, evaluation frameworks, long context handling, monitoring, and integration with Claude and Cursor.

videos.title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
videos.summary: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents are severely limited because they lack persistent context and memory across interactions, comparing them to an expert engineer with amnesia who can only see small portions of code. He advocates for fixing this fundamental architectural problem in agentic systems.

videos.title: The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
videos.summary: Ishaan Sehgal argues that agent identity and state should be anchored in session logs rather than in orchestration layers, tools, or LLMs. As models commoditize, durable append-only logs become the key differentiator, enabling durability, continuity, forkability, and portability across different runtimes and model providers.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
videos.summary: Cole Medin analyzes Google's 50-page playbook on agentic engineering, arguing that the AI model accounts for only 10% of results while the surrounding harness, context engineering, tools, and verification systems determine the other 90%. The video covers the spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering, the shift from conductor to orchestrator roles, and token economics implications for 2026 software development.

videos.title: My AI Memory Now Follows Me Across Every Tool!
videos.summary: Walrus Memory is a memory layer that persists outside individual AI agents, allowing context and memories to transfer between different tools and providers. The system encrypts memories before storage and grants users ownership and control over which agents can access them.

videos.title: The New Kimi K2.7 Model is VERY Impressive
videos.summary: Kimi K2.7 Code is an open-source, coding-focused agentic model developed by Moonshot AI. Cole Medin reviews it as impressive for its price and size.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: Advanced engineers use agentic loops that autonomously prompt and re-prompt language models without manual intervention, scaling horizontally through agent-to-agent orchestration. The approach enables parallel task execution but introduces cost and hallucination compounding challenges requiring observability harnesses.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: OpenClaw Creator's new secret project...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses OpenClaw creator's new project involving Crabbox and codebase harness setup available on GitHub. The project appears to involve tooling for AI development workflow, with access through AI Builder Club and Superdesign product.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: AI Jason explains Loop Engineer, a technique for building AI agents, and provides a step-by-step setup guide. Resources include a GitHub template, workshop materials, and the Superdesign tool for implementation.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video discusses how to build proactive AI agents and self-improving company systems. Covers proactive AI loops, autonomous optimization use cases like ads and SEO, and the role of memory and tools in enabling agent autonomy.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason demonstrates the /goal feature for autonomous task execution and /mission exploration for long-form horizontal tasks in development tools. The video covers setup, prompting strategies, and goal-buddy functionality as part of an emerging autonomous coder ecosystem.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: It's about time we learn Transformers..
videos.summary: A video tutorial explaining how generative pretrained transformers work under the hood. Covers self-attention, multi-head attention, feed forward networks, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections as core transformer components.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code explains Andrej Karpathy's bigram language model in a 10-minute tutorial covering tokenization, embeddings, and training. The bigram model serves as a pedagogical foundation for understanding larger language models like GPT, incorporating loss functions, softmax, and the AdamW optimizer.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series (Nano, Super, Ultra), featuring a hybrid architecture that combines Mamba transformers, Latent Mixture of Experts, and MTP. The video analyzes how these components differentiate Nemotron 3 from other open-source models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a language model featuring MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) that replaces full attention with sparse attention. The model includes tiled and I/O improvements to KV cache optimization, enabling continuous reads and reducing latency during prefill and decode stages.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Needs a Tool
videos.summary: Tool calling enables AI agents to request external tools for real-time data and actions rather than relying solely on training data. The video covers tool schema design, handler implementation, and how system prompts guide agents to select appropriate tools based on user requests.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS provides AI coding agents with up-to-date documentation, best practices, and tested procedures for working with AWS services. It improves agent success rates and reduces wasted effort by supplying guardrails and knowledge beyond general training data.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Developers demonstrated how to run Claude Code, an agentic coding tool by Anthropic, on Amazon Bedrock while maintaining data residency within AWS infrastructure. The tutorial covered environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, per-team cost tracking, and content governance via Bedrock Guardrails.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents strategies for tech job hunting in 2026 as AI screens resumes and automates hiring. The video argues that personal branding, networking, and hands-on project experience (building, blogging, open-source contribution) remain valuable, and that junior developers will continue to have a role as job categories evolve rather than disappear outright.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: YouTube video discussing whether Google engineers use "vibe coding" practices. Compares code generation approaches between OpenAI Codex and Claude, and examines Google's coding practices across 2024-2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: A YouTube video from Alberta Tech discussing Malus, a company or product, and its impact on open source software development. The video explores how Malus operates and questions whether it represents a threat to the open source ecosystem and its maintainers.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that code is only one component of software competitive advantage. The real moat comes from customer context, proprietary data, product experience, and operational scale. He contends that fears of AI destroying SaaS companies are overblown.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that a "ChatGPT moment" for robotics is arriving, but the field's focus on humanoid form factors is myopic. She contends that designing robots for human delight and interaction, rather than utility alone, will be critical as billions of robots enter society.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how to compete against legacy enterprise software vendors by building a full platform from day one rather than shipping incremental features. She covers why even Google relied on spreadsheets, the importance of earning customer trust through respected references, and a hiring philosophy focused on raising the bar across the company.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that real product-market fit manifests as customer pull rather than push, and that founders should judge PMF through embedded customer relationships rather than formal interviews. He warns that passionate small markets and enthusiastic single customers can create false signals of PMF.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: AI Could Delete EVERYTHING! Businesses Ignored This! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof argues that businesses have overlooked critical security threats posed by agentic AI, including prompt injection attacks, data deletion risks, and unauthorized actions. He contends that these risks are a primary reason businesses remain hesitant to deploy agentic AI despite its potential benefits.

videos.title: AI Podcast: Anthropic's Latest & Lisbon's Electric Vibe #shorts
videos.summary: A short-form podcast episode titled "This Is Tech Year" covers Anthropic's latest developments with guest Joao. The episode also references the Santos festival in the Lisbon area.

videos.title: Small Teams vs. Big Teams: Productivity & Meeting Madness! #shorts
videos.summary: Small agile teams are more productive than large teams, which get bogged down in meetings. The content argues that efficiency is achieved through smaller organizational units rather than larger ones.

videos.title: AI Memory Boom: Micron & SK Hynix Surge! Huge Growth Ahead! #shorts
videos.summary: The video discusses how AI demand is driving growth in high bandwidth memory markets. Micron and SK Hynix are identified as key companies benefiting from and shaping this memory boom.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
